meta.title: Petrovaradin Fortress Clock Tower (The Drunken Clock)｜Novi Sad's Iconic Landmark & Danube Panorama - Best European Spots
meta.description: Complete guide to Petrovaradin Fortress Clock Tower in Novi Sad, Serbia. Learn about opening hours, transportation, ticket prices, tour tips and stunning photos. The legendary 'Drunken Clock' with breathtaking Danube views.

hero.title: Petrovaradin Fortress Clock Tower
hero.subtitle: The Legendary 'Drunken Clock' & Danube River Panorama in Novi Sad, Serbia

intro.title: Discover the Charm of the Drunken Clock
intro.description:
Climb up more than two hundred stone steps, and when you finally catch your breath at the top of Petrovaradin Fortress, the famous black-and-white 'Drunken Clock' will stand right before your eyes. But what's even more breathtaking is the unobstructed panoramic view that stretches out before you.

From here, the Danube River flows gracefully at your feet, its broad surface reflecting the colors of the sky. Across the river, the red-roofed buildings, church spires, and modern bridges of Novi Sad's old town unfold in a magnificent tableau.

The clock tower was designed with remarkable tenderness: the long hand points to the hours so that Danube sailors and townspeople across the river could tell the time at a glance, even from a distance. As you listen to the ancient mechanism's ticking and sip coffee at an outdoor café nearby, you'll understand why Novi Sad was chosen as the European Capital of Culture.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Strongly recommended to arrive half an hour before sunset for the best views of Novi Sad's skyline and Danube sunset
The fortress and clock tower exterior are free to visit, no admission fee required
The fortress top has no shade in summer, sun protection is essential
The cobblestone steps can be slippery in rain or snow, wear anti-slip shoes

highlights.title: Highlights
items: 'Gibraltar of the Danube' and symbol of Novi Sad
'Drunken Clock' - unique design with long hand for hours, short hand for minutes
18th-century Baroque architecture, white tower with red walls
Venue of EXIT Festival, iconic backdrop of European Capital of Culture

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.officialNameValue: Sahat kula / Pijani sat (Clock Tower / Drunken Clock)
basicInfo.typeValue: Historic Landmark, Panoramic City Viewpoint, Baroque Military Architecture

hours.title: Opening Hours
hours.fortressDesc: Open 24 hours, 7 days a week, no access restrictions
hours.tip: Strongly recommended to arrive half an hour before sunset for the best views of Novi Sad's skyline and Danube sunset.

tickets.title: Ticket Prices
tickets.fortressDesc: The fortress grounds and clock tower exterior are free to visit. No admission fee required.
tickets.catacombsDesc: Separate tickets required for the underground military tunnels (Catacombs) or Novi Sad City Museum.
tickets.catacombsNote: Guided tours are strongly recommended for the underground tunnels.

walking.title: Walking (Highly Recommended)
walking.desc: Starting from Danube Park in central Novi Sad, walk east across the Varadin Bridge over the Danube, then climb the steps (~214 steps) at the fortress base. The 20-30 minute walk leads directly to the top observation platform with the clock tower.

bus.title: Bus
bus.desc: Take bus lines 3, 9, 61, 62, or 64 across the river. Get off at the first stop after the bridge (at the fortress base) and walk up the hill.

car.title: Car / Taxi
car.desc: Vehicles can drive up the winding mountain road to the fortress. Free or paid parking is available near the clock tower. Taxis from central Novi Sad take only 10 minutes to the fortress top.
